name: The Astrologer
description:
Welcome. Do you wish to know the secret of the Soul, written in the Stars?

The Astrologer is in, maybe she'll be able to help you.

Technically, this isn't a character in the strictest sense (hence the Assistant tag). It started as a private resource I used to analyze star charts for the characters I create (or play with!) in order to flesh them out better (and to not bog the main chat).

However, her avatar slaps, so...why not share? Have fun too, and if you want to play her as an actual bot, be my guest, just write in memory a little description like you'd use for a persona and go forth!

HOW DOES THIS WORK?

Paste a star/birth chart. You can find plenty of free resources that calculate it starting from a birth date (my personal pick: Cafe's Astrology. It's free, and it generates an extremely detailed chart). The more complete, the better. The Astrologer will analyze it. If you want more star charts to be analyzed and compared, just ask her.

Alternatively (useful for character creation!), just describe the personality of a character and see what she has to say. Maybe your character is totally a Scorpio, like you predicted, but that Mars in Cancer gives them a nuance that you hadn't expected but fits well!

Small warning: I've tested her with Deepseek and GLM, and she can't generate an accurate birth chart. So, even if you know that your character/the person you're analyzing was born on the Xth day of MM month in YYYY year and at HH:MM in XZ location...well, it's likely she won't give you a real chart. Similarly, when building a chart from a personality, it's very likely the chart won't actually correspond to a real date. Again, I'd use a dedicated external resource and use her hints to generate an actual start chart, trying to go as close as possible to what she suggested. Or...just use her insights for character building, without generating an actual chart.

Just have fun! Astrology is nice, but it's not science under any possible meaning of the term. Like Tarots, studying it is interesting for its symbolism and cultural impact, not because it’s a hard guide to what you are and what you will be.

This is a toy to have fun and/or to assist a creative process, nothing more.
creator: clumsyProphet
